citation.anchor: 128, 64-bit general purpose registers

preconditions: A 96-register current frame makes logical r32-r127 accessible
PSR.bn remains in the ordinary bank-zero view
inputs: A distinct signed-imm8 value equal to each logical register number r1-r127
expected_results: A second guest pass reads every register and compares it with its unique literal
The final debugger image independently repeats all 127 comparisons
allowed_results: Every r1-r127 value equals its register number and all associated NaT bits are clear
cases.committed_prefix: Each stopped write and later stopped comparison commits independently
cases.restart_action: Not exercised for legal current-frame operands

oracle.derivation: The register number itself is the literal value; a separately encoded compare pass and host image parser must both reproduce the identity mapping

limitations: Rotating GR renaming and interruption bank switching are separate REG-005/REG-009 closure units and are not claimed here

citation.anchor: 128, 82-bit floating-point registers

preconditions: PSR.dfl and PSR.dfh are clear
FR0/FR1 constants are covered by REG-004
inputs: A distinct canonical integer significand equal to each writable index f2-f127
expected_results: After initializing the entire writable file, six chunks read every f2-f127 through GETF.SIG
allowed_results: Every observed significand equals its FR index
cases.committed_prefix: Each stopped SETF and GETF commits independently
cases.restart_action: Not exercised for enabled legal FR operands

result_observations: 126 exact GETF.SIG results across six processes

oracle.derivation: Each FR is assigned its literal index through SETF.SIG; complete-file initialization before every chunk detects aliased destinations or sources

limitations: This row covers the rrb.fr-zero index space; rotating renaming is a separate REG-010 closure unit

citation.anchor: 64, one-bit predicate registers

required_unchanged_state: GRs outside declared setup and results
cases.committed_prefix: Each packed predicate image commits as one stopped transfer
cases.restart_action: Not exercised

limitations: This row covers the rrb.pr-zero broadside view; rotating renaming is a separate REG-010 closure unit

citation.anchor: 8, 64-bit branch registers

citation.anchor: An interruption selects bank 0

preconditions: Execution begins in bank 0
PSR.ic is set before provoking the precise interruption
The interruption vector saves IPSR.bn and enters with PSR.bn clear
inputs: Distinct values in every banked GR16-GR31 of both banks
A NaT in bank-0 r16 and a NaT in bank-1 r31
A predicated illegal equal-target compare in slot 1
expected_results: The interruption handler observes bank 0 while IPSR.bn records bank 1
RFI restores bank 1 and suppressing the faulting predicate resumes at the interrupted bundle
BSW.0 returns to the original bank-0 values
Both banks preserve their independent NaT state
allowed_results: Exact one-to-one value and NaT preservation for all GR16-GR31 in both banks
permitted_state_changes: Both banked GR16-GR31 files and their NaT bits
PSR.bn, IPSR.bn, IIP, predicates, and declared observation GRs
expected_faults: Illegal Operation fault from the predicated equal-target compare
fault_priority: The illegal-operation fault is the sole enabled interruption source
cases.committed_prefix: Slot 0 commits before the slot-1 precise fault; slot 2 does not commit until RFI resumes with its predicate clear
cases.restart_action: The handler clears the qualifying predicate and RFI resumes the interrupted bundle in bank 1
applicable_variants: all sixteen values and NaT endpoints per bank

result_observations: full final bank-0 HMP GR and NaT image

oracle.derivation: The two banks receive disjoint arithmetic sequences and opposite NaT endpoints; handler and resumed code independently validate the architecturally selected view

limitations: Nested interruptions, taken-branch interruption collection, and MP bank visibility are separate closure units

endpoint comparisons after every intermediate step

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0
PSR.ic is enabled for precise collected-fault restart
RSC.mode is zero
A guest CPUID[4] read confirms ru bit 3 is clear, making optional AR45 reserved

M-unit and I-unit register-form reads and writes
Enabled p1 and false p1 qualifications
Zero enabled-write value, nonzero false-write value, and poison read destination
expected_results: All 128 selectors match the independent Table 3-3 M-only, I-only, reserved, or ignored partition
Every legal enabled read/write completes
Every reserved or wrong-unit enabled access takes precise Illegal Operation
Both ignored ranges are legal in either unit, discard writes, and read zero
allowed_results: Exactly the Table 3-3 selector/unit classification for all 1,024 read/write/qualification combinations
permitted_state_changes: BSPSTORE/RNAT/RSC state from legal zero writes
required_unchanged_state: False-qualified access destinations and architectural AR state
fault_priority: Selector/unit/read-only legality is evaluated only after the qualifying predicate is true
cases.committed_prefix: Each selector access is isolated by stops; a fault commits only its completed setup prefix and leaves the destination poisoned
cases.restart_action: The common handler validates IIP and ISR, clears p1, and RFI re-executes the same access nullified before continuing
applicable_variants: defined, reserved, ignored, read-only, and wrong-unit classes

limitations: Nonzero field masks, CPL/PSR.si privilege, RSE-mode restrictions, and named AR value semantics remain separate REG-007/REG-008 closure units

preconditions: Every caller frame is complete
Direct and indirect targets are bundle aligned
The architectural call-frame case starts with literal CFM SOF6/SOL4, EC42, CPL3, two dirty outputs, and one output NaT
inputs: B3 direct call, X4 long call, and B5 indirect call
Forward, backward, far, equal-fallthrough, forwarded, and aliased targets
p0, same-group true, stopped false, nullified, and first-taken-suppressed calls
One-bundle-per-TB and combined execution
expected_results: A taken call overwrites PFS with the exact old CFM, EC, and CPL image
The link receives bundle address plus 16
CFM becomes the callee frame with output value/NaT and RSE mappings preserved
Live EC and CPL are unchanged
A false or suppressed call preserves PFS, link, CFM, and RSE state
Split and combined execution are identical
allowed_results: Exactly twelve literal call-program outcomes and one focused typed call helper site per encoded call
permitted_state_changes: ALAT state associated with the caller frame
required_unchanged_state: Live EC and PSR.cpl on a taken call
PFS, link, CFM, RSE, and caller state for every false or suppressed call
Dirty output value and NaT identity across the frame remap
cases.committed_prefix: A selected call publishes link, PFS, CFM, and RSE effects as one typed control-flow transition; an earlier taken branch suppresses them all
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for complete-frame, aligned-target call inputs

oracle.derivation: Expected PFS fields, link address, callee frame, and output remapping are calculated from literal caller state and the published call rules; split-versus-combined equality is an additional execution-shape invariant

limitations: Incomplete-frame call behavior is architecturally undefined
Taken-branch traps and call-target translation faults remain separate BR/EXC/MMU rows

citation.anchor: When a br.ret is executed, the PFS is copied to the CFM and EC

preconditions: Each PFS image defines a valid caller frame
The dynamic branch target is bundle aligned
Clean-frame and four-word incomplete SOL4 backing-store images are constructed independently
inputs: p0, true, and false-qualified returns
Same-group forwarded BR and PFS values versus ordinary group-entry reads
Clean and incomplete frames spanning an RNAT slot
Taken-branch and lower-privilege post-target traps
First-register and RNAT SoftMMU fill faults with RFI retry
Fresh-process save/load at the handler RFI
expected_results: A taken return restores exact CFM and EC from the selected PFS and aligns the dynamic target
False qualification preserves frame and EC
Same-group reads retain ordinary entry images while the return consumes forwarded BR/PFS
Mandatory fills restore exact values and NaTs across the RNAT slot
Post-target traps collect restored state and lower privilege has priority
Fill faults expose exact IIP/IIPA/IFA/ISR and retry idempotently through IFS.v=0 RFI
Fresh-process continuation equals direct execution
allowed_results: Exactly ten literal return-program outcomes, including clean, trap, mandatory-fill, fill-fault/retry, and migration paths
permitted_state_changes: PSR.cpl when the PFS does not request a prohibited privilege increase
RSE frame mapping and mandatory-fill state
required_unchanged_state: Frame, EC, and RSE state for a false-qualified return
Backing-store words except the deliberate fault-handler replacement
Already committed fill prefix across retry
cases.committed_prefix: A taken return restores PFS state before target traps; mandatory fills commit an exact prefix and fault-word retry does not duplicate it
cases.restart_action: The handler clears saved translation state and RFI resumes with IFS.v=0, retrying the faulting word; one checkpoint repeats this in a fresh QEMU process

oracle.derivation: Expected frame fields, privilege/trap order, RSE addresses, RNAT placement, fill prefix, and retry results are computed from literal PFS/backing images and the published return/RSE rules without importing production helpers

limitations: Reserved or oversized bad-PFS return faults, arbitrary fill lengths, access rights, memory attributes, nested interruptions, and MP ordering remain separate REG/RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: the BSP pointer is set to the address presented plus the size of the dirty partition plus the size of any intervening NaT collections

preconditions: RSC.mode is zero
Every current and caller frame is complete
Real equal-fallthrough br.call transitions grow one dirty local per frame
Every presented BSPSTORE address is implemented and mapped as architectural state
inputs: Every dirty-partition size from zero through 96 registers
Every BSPSTORE address offset from zero through 63 register slots
All 6,208 Cartesian size/offset combinations
expected_results: BSPSTORE and RSE.BspLoad equal the aligned presented address
BSP equals the aligned address plus eight bytes per dirty register plus eight bytes per intervening RNAT collection
The exact independent formula is base + 8 * (dirty + floor((offset + dirty) / 63))
allowed_results: Exactly one BSP value for each of the 6,208 combinations; post-write RNAT contents are undefined
permitted_state_changes: Declared GR, predicate, and call-frame state
cases.committed_prefix: Each stopped call grows one dirty register; each stopped BSPSTORE write atomically rebases the three RSE pointers
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for complete frames and implemented mapped addresses
applicable_variants: zero, single, and double RNAT crossings

oracle.derivation: The expected pointer is calculated from the published 63-register plus one-RNAT layout using literal dirty count and address slot; no production RSE helper or state is imported

limitations: Clean-partition invalidation, undefined RNAT payload, backing-store faults, interruption boundaries, and migration remain separate RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: After this instruction completes execution BSPSTORE is equal to BSP

preconditions: RSC.mode is zero
A complete 96-register dirty partition contains distinct values one through 96
Alternating dirty registers carry NaT
BSPSTORE starts at mapped writable offset 63 and UNAT starts at 0x5555555555555555
inputs: One legal unpredicated flushrs encoded in slot zero as the first instruction in its group
A 96-register dirty partition crossing two RNAT collection positions
expected_results: All 96 dirty values are stored exactly once and in architectural backing-store order while RNAT slots are skipped
The completed RNAT word is 0x5555555555555555 and the final partial AR.RNAT image is 0x00000000aaaaaaaa
BSPSTORE equals BSP at 0x1d08 after completion
RSE.BspLoad may remain at the original 0x19f8 or advance to 0x1d08
allowed_results: The two architecturally permitted BspLoad values with the one exact memory, RNAT, BSP, and BSPSTORE image
permitted_state_changes: The 96 backing-store register words and completed RNAT collection
required_unchanged_state: Distinct dirty register payload and NaT order
Memory outside the declared backing-store image
cases.committed_prefix: Each mandatory RSE store commits in backing-store order; successful completion empties the dirty partition and makes BSPSTORE equal BSP
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for the valid mapped writable backing store

oracle.derivation: Expected data and NaT positions are enumerated directly from literal values, address slots, and the published 63-register RNAT layout; the production spill helper is not imported

limitations: Fault and retry behavior, interrupt boundaries, other traffic lengths, clean-partition fills, and migration remain separate RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: A new stack frame of zero size is allocated which does not include any registers from the previous frame

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with a complete SOF8/SOL3/SOR8 frame
All eight locals and outputs contain distinct values
All three rename bases are nonzero after one WTOP
The backing store is mapped and writable
inputs: One legal unpredicated B8 cover last in its instruction group
PSR.ic clear and set variants
A following legal flushrs that makes the preserved frame visible
expected_results: CFM becomes exactly zero and BSP advances by all eight current-frame registers
All eight local and output values enter the dirty partition and flush in order
With PSR.ic clear IFS receives the exact old CFM and IFS.v=1
With PSR.ic set the established IFS image is unchanged
allowed_results: Exactly the two collection-policy images with one common zero CFM, pointer state, and eight-word backing image
permitted_state_changes: IFS only when PSR.ic is clear
Declared backing-store words and observation registers
cases.committed_prefix: COVER atomically preserves the complete current frame and publishes the zero frame at the group boundary; the later flush commits each dirty word in order
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for complete frames and mapped writable backing storage

oracle.derivation: The old CFM image and eight-word BSP advance are calculated from literal SOF/SOL/SOR and one published WTOP rotation; no production frame-preservation helper is imported

limitations: Virtualization, incomplete-frame undefined behavior, CPL1-CPL3 execution, interruption boundaries, ALAT observation, and migration remain separate RSE/EXC/ALT/SYS rows

citation.anchor: the register rename base registers (CFM.rrb.gr, CFM.rrb.fr, and CFM.rrb.pr) are cleared

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with a complete SOF8/SOL8/SOR8 frame
One WTOP establishes RRB.GR=7, RRB.FR=95, and RRB.PR=47
The rotating GR region contains distinct values one through eight
inputs: One legal unpredicated clrrrb.pr last in its instruction group
One legal unpredicated all-form clrrrb last in its instruction group
expected_results: clrrrb.pr clears only RRB.PR and preserves RRB.GR=7, RRB.FR=95, and the rotated GR view
all-form clrrrb clears all three bases and exposes the base-zero GR view
SOF, SOL, and SOR remain unchanged
allowed_results: Exactly one CFM and r32/r33 image for each of the two forms
permitted_state_changes: Logical rotating register views implied by the new bases
cases.committed_prefix: Each B8 form changes only its defined rename bases at the terminating group boundary
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for legal last-in-group encodings

oracle.derivation: The expected bases follow the literal B8 pseudocode; the before/after logical GR values are derived from one modulo-eight WTOP rotation

limitations: CPL1-CPL3 execution, undefined nonterminal placement, complete FR/PR logical-image observation, ALAT effects, and migration remain separate RSE/BND/ALT/SYS rows

citation.anchor: After loadrs completes, all registers and NaT collections between the current BSP and the tear-point

preconditions: RSC.mode is zero and the current frame is complete and empty
A prior flush leaves 96 clean registers spanning two RNAT boundaries
The replacement backing image is mapped and readable
inputs: Zero-distance loadrs to invalidate every stacked register outside the current frame
A full-span loadrs with ignored low loadrs bits set
Ninety-six replacement payloads, one completed RNAT word, and one partial AR.RNAT image
expected_results: Zero-distance loadrs makes BSPSTORE and BspLoad equal BSP and invalidates the old clean image
Full-span loadrs fetches exactly the tear-point-to-BSP payload/RNAT image and marks it dirty
BSP is unchanged while BSPSTORE and BspLoad equal the aligned tear point
Destroying memory after loadrs and flushing reconstructs all 96 replacement values and both NaT classes exactly
allowed_results: One exact reconstructed backing image; after the diagnostic flush BspLoad may preserve the tear point or advance to BSP
permitted_state_changes: Dirty, clean, and invalid RSE partitions
required_unchanged_state: Memory outside the declared backing-store span
cases.committed_prefix: Zero-distance invalidation completes first; each mandatory load commits in descending backing-store order and the diagnostic flush later commits the reconstructed dirty image
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for the valid mapped readable and writable backing store

oracle.derivation: Register addresses, tear point, RNAT positions, replacement payloads, and NaT bitmaps are generated from literal 63-register collection rules without importing the production loader

limitations: Other load distances, clean/dirty tear-point relations, memory faults and retry, interruption boundaries, big-endian RSC mode, CPL1-CPL3 execution, and migration remain separate RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: This instruction will fault with an Illegal Operation fault under any of the following conditions

preconditions: Each case executes at CPL0 with PSR.ic enabled before the faulting M25 slot
The over-capacity case establishes an implemented BSP address without touching its backing memory
inputs: Nonzero RSC.mode with zero load distance
Nonzero CFM.sof with nonzero load distance
A load distance containing 97 register words after excluding RNAT slots
expected_results: Each case raises exact Illegal Operation in slot zero before memory traffic
The rejected operation preserves CFM, BSP, BSPSTORE, and BspLoad
The over-capacity preflight counts register words rather than RNAT collection words
allowed_results: Exactly three General Exception vector entries with ISR zero and the literal faulting IIP
permitted_state_changes: Architectural interruption collection state and declared observation registers
cases.committed_prefix: Stopped setup state commits; LOADRS preflight rejects the request before the first mandatory word
cases.restart_action: The test stops at exact collection; repair-and-retry of memory traffic is a separate RSE-007 row

oracle.derivation: The three illegal predicates are transcribed from M25 pseudocode; the 97-register span is counted independently while skipping addresses whose slot index is 63

limitations: Undefined non-first-in-group placement, memory fault classes and retry, interruption boundaries, CPL1-CPL3 execution, and migration remain separate BND/RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: The change of frame size is immediate. The write of GR r1 and subsequent instructions in the same instruction group use the new frame

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with RSC.mode zero
The backing store used to isolate legal cases is mapped and writable
AR.PFS contains the legal distinguishable value 85
inputs: Three hundred thirty-two legal vectors covering every SOF0-96, SOL0-96, encoded SOR0-12, and destination r1-r127 value
Every unchanged-SOF8 SOL partition and a zero-base SOR transition
An ALLOC followed in the same group by a write to newly in-frame r127
expected_results: Every destination receives old AR.PFS with NaT clear
IFS after a diagnostic COVER exposes the exact SOF/SOL/SOR CFM for every vector
Allocated overlapping values and NaTs survive repartitioning
The instruction following ALLOC sees the new SOF96 mapping in the same group
allowed_results: One exact destination/PFS/NaT and CFM image for every legal vector; undefined newly allocated values are never asserted
permitted_state_changes: The selected destination GR and its NaT
RSE partitions and declared diagnostic backing storage
required_unchanged_state: All overlapping allocated value/NaT pairs except the selected destination
CFM rename bases during legal matrix rows
cases.committed_prefix: ALLOC is first in each group; the frame change and destination write are visible to later instructions in that group
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for valid fields and mapped backing storage
applicable_variants: SOR 0 through 96 in multiples of eight

oracle.derivation: Legal tuples are generated directly from the published 7-bit SOF/SOL, 4-bit SOR, and 7-bit r1 domains; expected CFM fields are packed arithmetically and exposed through architectural COVER rather than a production ALLOC helper

limitations: Undefined newly allocated values, non-first-in-group placement, privilege variants, ALAT details, mandatory-store faults/retry, interruption boundaries, and migration remain separate RSE/BND/ALT/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: illegal combinations can be encoded in the instruction

preconditions: Each fault case enables and serializes PSR.ic
Illegal fields are tested from a zero frame
The SOR-change case establishes nonzero GR, FR, and PR rename bases through one WTOP
inputs: r0 and both low/high out-of-frame stacked destinations
qp1 and qp63
SOF97 and SOF127
low/high SOL greater than SOF
four SOR-greater-than-SOF boundaries
A legal SOR change while rename bases are nonzero
expected_results: Each of thirteen statically illegal partitions raises precise Illegal Operation with ISR zero
A SOR change with nonzero rename bases raises Reserved Register/Field with ISR code 0x30
Every rejected ALLOC preserves CFM, all RSE pointers, and its suffix destination
allowed_results: Exactly thirteen Illegal Operation records and one Reserved Register/Field record at the literal slot-zero IIP
permitted_state_changes: Architectural interruption collection state and declared observation registers
cases.committed_prefix: Stopped setup commits; ALLOC preflight rejects the operation before frame or backing-store state changes
cases.restart_action: The test stops at exact collection; memory fault repair/retry is a separate RSE-007 row

oracle.derivation: The illegal equivalence classes and priority order are transcribed from M34 pseudocode and checked against literal field arithmetic; no production validator is imported

limitations: Undefined non-first-in-group placement, every cross-product of simultaneously illegal fields, mandatory-store memory faults/retry, privilege variants, interruption boundaries, and migration remain separate BND/RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: If insufficient registers are available to allocate the desired frame alloc will stall the processor until enough dirty registers are written to the backing store

preconditions: Ninety-six real one-local call frames occupy the complete dirty physical register stack
Each dirty register has a distinct payload and alternating NaT
The backing store starts at RNAT-relative offset 63 and is mapped writable
inputs: One legal SOF96/SOL96 ALLOC targeting static r31
A full physical stack requiring all 96 old dirty registers to be stored before allocation can complete
expected_results: ALLOC completes with exact SOF96/SOL96 CFM and old AR.PFS in r31 with NaT clear
BSPSTORE advances to BSP after all 96 mandatory stores
All payloads occupy exact register backing slots
The completed RNAT word and partial AR.RNAT image contain the exact alternating NaTs
allowed_results: One exact 96-register/RNAT backing image and final pointer state
required_unchanged_state: Memory outside the declared backing-store span
cases.committed_prefix: Mandatory RSE stores commit in backing-store order before the frame growth and destination become architecturally complete
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for mapped writable backing storage; mandatory-store faults and retry are a separate row

oracle.derivation: Backing addresses and RNAT bits are enumerated from the published 63-register collection layout using literal values; the production spill path is not imported

limitations: Other spill counts and starting offsets are represented by the separately closed dirty BSPSTORE matrix; mandatory-store translation/access faults, restart, interruption boundaries, privilege variants, and migration remain separate RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: Mandatory RSE stores occur in the context of alloc and flushrs instructions only. Any faults raised by these instructions are delivered on the issuing instruction. Faults raised by mandatory RSE loads caused by a loadrs are delivered on the issuing instruction.

preconditions: Each case executes at CPL0 with PSR.ic and PSR.rt enabled and serialized
One 4 KiB identity DTC page admits a strict mandatory-word prefix while the adjacent page is absent
ALLOC, FLUSHRS, and LOADRS fields and complete-frame preconditions are legal
inputs: A ninety-register dirty partition whose SOF8 ALLOC spills one register and one RNAT word before a second-register miss
A four-register dirty partition whose FLUSHRS stores two registers and one RNAT word before a third-register miss
A five-word LOADRS span whose two high-page registers load before an RNAT-address miss
expected_results: Every fault is Alternate Data TLB at the issuing instruction with exact IIP, IFA, IFS.v zero, ISR.rs, and read/write classification
BSPSTORE names the uncommitted fault boundary after the exact successful prefix
The physical handler installs the missing identity DTC entry without clearing saved PSR.rt and poisons every committed prefix word
RFI retry preserves the poison, completes only the suffix, and publishes the exact final ALLOC frame, FLUSHRS image, or LOADRS-reflushed image
allowed_results: Exactly three repaired Alternate Data TLB faults followed by exact terminal states; no committed word may be replayed
permitted_state_changes: Architectural interruption collection state and declared observation registers
required_unchanged_state: Every memory word outside the declared backing spans
Already committed register/RNAT prefix words after handler poisoning
cases.committed_prefix: Every successful mandatory word advances BSPSTORE before the next word; the faulting word and ALLOC final frame do not commit
cases.restart_action: The handler uses ITC.D to install the missing page and executes RFI with saved PSR.rt intact; the issuing instruction resumes from BSPSTORE and must not replay the poisoned prefix

oracle.derivation: Fault addresses, RNAT slots, store/load order, committed pointer boundaries, and expected payloads are calculated from the published 63-register backing-store layout and rse_store/rse_load success rules; no production RSE helper is imported

limitations: This row closes Alternate Data TLB repair/retry at CPL0 only; protection, unaligned, unsupported-attribute, other translation classes, external-interrupt boundaries, migration, and CPL1-CPL3 remain separate RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: After the translation entry is loaded, additional TLB faults are checked

preconditions: Each case enables and serializes PSR.ic and PSR.rt, plus PSR.pk for key checks
A legal prefix page admits an exact mandatory-word prefix and the adjacent page carries one selected protection condition
FLUSHRS and LOADRS fields and complete-frame preconditions are legal
inputs: Seven mandatory-store variants: Page Not Present, NaTPage, Key Miss, Key Permission, Access Rights, Access Bit, and Dirty Bit
Six mandatory-load variants: the same classes except store-only Dirty Bit
RSC.pl three for the dedicated load-rights denial; architecturally valid keyed ITIR and PKR values for key cases
expected_results: Each fault occurs on the issuing instruction with its exact vector, IIP, IFA, IFS.v zero, ISR.rs, read/write classification, and NaTPage code
BSPSTORE names the uncommitted boundary after the exact successful prefix
The physical handler replaces only the denying PTE or PKR and poisons every committed prefix word
RFI preserves saved PSR.rt and PSR.pk, retains all poison, completes only the suffix, and publishes the exact final backing image and pointers
allowed_results: Exactly thirteen repaired protection faults followed by exact terminal states; no committed word may be replayed
permitted_state_changes: The repaired DTC entry or PKR
Architectural interruption collection state and declared observation registers
required_unchanged_state: Saved PSR.rt and PSR.pk across RFI
Every memory word outside the declared backing spans
Already committed register/RNAT prefix words after handler poisoning
cases.committed_prefix: Every successful mandatory word advances BSPSTORE before the next word; the faulting word and final instruction state do not commit
cases.restart_action: The handler installs a permissive replacement PTE or valid enabled PKR and executes RFI with saved translation/key state intact; the issuing instruction resumes from BSPSTORE without replaying the poisoned prefix

oracle.derivation: The seven-class priority, PTE/PKR conditions, RSC.pl denial, fault addresses, RNAT slots, committed pointer boundaries, and expected payloads are calculated from the published translation and RSE rules; no production translation or RSE helper is imported

limitations: ALLOC uses the same mandatory-store path and has separate DTC retry evidence but is not repeated for every protection class; unaligned and unsupported-attribute reachability, other translation classes, external-interrupt boundaries, migration, and nonzero guest CPL remain separate RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows

citation.anchor: If PSR.dd is 1, Data Debug faults are disabled for one instruction or one mandatory RSE memory reference

preconditions: Each case enables and serializes PSR.ic, PSR.rt, and PSR.db
DBR0 and DBR1 select the mandatory RSE address, RSC.pl, and access direction
The priority cases select one translated protection denial on the matched address
The one-shot cases enter with PSR.dd set and a broad matching DBR
inputs: Thirteen protection-to-Data-Debug chains covering every applicable store/load protection class
One two-reference FLUSHRS store sequence and one two-reference LOADRS load sequence under PSR.dd
Exact DBR matches for priority and mask-zero broad DBR matches for suppression
expected_results: Each protection fault occurs before Data Debug on the same issuing instruction and mandatory address
The first handler repairs only translation/protection and the second disables the DBR
Both RFI retries preserve the exact handler-poisoned committed prefix
PSR.dd suppresses exactly one successful matching mandatory reference, clears, and exposes Data Debug on the next reference
Every Data Debug fault reports vector 0x5900, ISR.rs one, the exact access bit, IFS.v zero, and the issuing IIP/faulting IFA
allowed_results: Exactly fifteen terminal program images: thirteen two-fault priority/retry chains and two one-fault PSR.dd suppression/retry cases
permitted_state_changes: Architectural interruption collection state and declared observation registers
required_unchanged_state: Saved PSR.rt and PSR.db across RFI
Every memory word outside the declared backing spans
Already committed register/RNAT prefix words after handler poisoning
fault_priority: Data Debug matching and PSR.dd suppression
cases.committed_prefix: Protection and Data Debug interruptions preserve the exact already committed prefix; handler poisoning proves neither RFI path replays it
cases.restart_action: The protection handler repairs the PTE or PKR and RFI-retries; the Data Debug handler disables DBR1 and RFI-retries. PSR.dd clears only after the first successful mandatory reference.

result_observations: protection and Data Debug exception kind/vector/source

oracle.derivation: DBR address/mask/PLM/access images, fault priority, PSR.dd one-reference lifetime, RNAT addresses, committed boundaries, and expected payloads are derived directly from the cited architectural rules and literal inputs; no production debug, translation, or RSE helper is imported

limitations: ALLOC uses the same mandatory-store debug path but is not redundantly expanded here; virtual-mode DBR interpretation is exercised while physical-mode DBR matching, instruction breakpoints, external-interrupt boundaries, migration, nonzero guest CPL, and unaligned/unsupported-attribute reachability remain separate RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS rows
